location.origin: Washington, D.C. and Augusta, Georgia (raised between both). Born November 30, 1977. Attended Georgetown Visitation Preparatory School in D.C. (gospel choir). Moved to New York City at 17 for Fordham University at Lincoln Center (English and history degree). Performed with Moomtez and the Black Rock Coalition while at Fordham.
location.formative: D.C.: gospel, pop, soul, go-go (Chuck Brown tradition). Augusta, GA: rural Southern influence. Georgetown Visitation: gospel choir foundation for four-octave vocal range. Fordham University: humanities training (English + history), Black Rock Coalition membership (genre freedom thesis), backup singing for local bands.
location.career: Brooklyn, New York (primary career base through early 2010s). Debut For Lovers, Dreamers & Me (2006, BBE Records). Grammy nomination Best Urban/Alternative Performance for 'Dream' (2008). Signed to Epic Records (Sony) post-Grammy. Album shelved 2007-2012 during executive restructuring. She released March 19, 2013 (Kickstarter-funded, Brooklyn Recordings). Prince covered 'Another Love' on Plectrumelectrum (2014, 3rdeyegirl). Nina Simone tribute 'I Put a Spell on You' (2015). Mystery EP (October 2019, self-released). Relocated to Los Angeles. Sinners soundtrack with Miles Caton -- 'Last Time (I Seen the Sun)' (2025, Sony Masterworks). 98th Academy Awards performance 'I Lied to You' (March 15, 2026) with Raphael Saadiq, Brittany Howard, Buddy Guy, Christone Kingfish Ingram, Misty Copeland, Bobby Rush. TV placements: Gotham, Being Mary Jane, Empire, Black Lightning, Entourage, The L Word. Live: Apollo Theater, Lincoln Theater D.C., BET Black Girls Rock (2013), NAACP Image Awards (2016), NPR Tiny Desk Concert.

labels: BBE Records (debut, 2006, independent UK label -- Barely Breaking Even)
Epic Records (Sony Music, post-Grammy signing ~2007, album shelved, departed ~2012)
Sony Masterworks (Sinners soundtrack 2025 -- collaborative, not signed)

members.name: Alice Smith
members.note: Four-octave vocal range. Grammy-nominated (2008). Styles: neo-soul, rock, blues, jazz, gospel, cabaret, art-pop. Cited influences: Patti LaBelle, Billie Holiday, Fiona Apple, Anita Baker. Rolling Stone '10 Artists to Watch' 2006. Prince covered her song 'Another Love' (2014). Oscar performer (2026). Black Rock Coalition member. Daughter Lula with Citizen Cope.

associated_musicians.name: Prince
associated_musicians.note: Recorded interpretation on Plectrumelectrum with 3rdeyegirl. Peer recognition from one of the most genre-fluid artists in recorded music history. Prince's own practice (funk, rock, pop, soul, jazz, psychedelia) mirrors Smith's genre-fluid approach.

associated_musicians.name: Citizen Cope
associated_musicians.note: Recording artist. Father of Smith's daughter Lula. Brooklyn-based singer-songwriter.

associated_musicians.name: Miles Caton
associated_musicians.note: Co-wrote and performed 'Last Time (I Seen the Sun)' for Sinners soundtrack (2025). Performed together at 98th Academy Awards (2026).

associated_musicians.name: Raphael Saadiq
associated_musicians.note: Performed 'I Lied to You' ensemble at 98th Academy Awards. Neo-soul/R&B producer-artist. Former Tony! Toni! Tone!

associated_musicians.name: Brittany Howard
associated_musicians.note: Alabama Shakes frontwoman. Performed guitar at 98th Academy Awards alongside Smith.

associated_musicians.name: Buddy Guy
associated_musicians.note: Blues legend. Part of Oscar performance ensemble for Sinners.

associated_musicians.name: Aloe Blacc
associated_musicians.note: Collaborated on 'Baby' for Red Hot + Rio 2 (2011).

associated_musicians.name: Doyle Bramhall II
associated_musicians.note: Performed 'Cry' together at Eric Clapton's Crossroads Guitar Festival, Madison Square Garden (2013).

associated_musicians.name: Vernon Reid / Black Rock Coalition
associated_musicians.note: Smith performed with the Black Rock Coalition at Fordham University. BRC founded by Vernon Reid (Living Colour), Greg Tate, and Konda Mason (1985). Genre-freedom thesis for Black musicians.

theory_nodes.concept: The shelved frequency (forty-first acoustic element -- the sound of music that was recorded, completed, and suppressed by institutional power before reaching an audience; the engine's first negative acoustic element, defined by absence rather than presence; Alice Smith's Epic Records recordings 2007-2012 exist in full fidelity but were never released; the shelved frequency is the acoustic signature of institutional suppression)
examples: Alice Smith (degree 40) -- entire album's worth of material recorded for Epic Records 2007-2012, shelved during label executive restructuring. Songs existed in completed form but were legally blocked from release.

Contrast with silence (degree 0, Quincy Jones) -- silence is the absence of sound by design. The shelved frequency is the presence of sound that has been blocked from transmission. Silence is chosen; the shelved frequency is imposed.
Contrast with the Pacific crossing (degree 39) -- a geographic-timbral element. The shelved frequency is an institutional-timbral element.

Universal pattern: Fiona Apple's Extraordinary Machine (shelved by Epic), Prince's vault (accumulated during Warner Bros. dispute), Lauryn Hill's unreleased sophomore. The shelved frequency is the acoustic element acknowledging the music industry's capacity to suppress the art it claims to support.
theory_nodes.description: The shelved frequency is the forty-first acoustic element in the Seattle 360 engine. It describes the acoustic quality of music that has been recorded, mixed, and completed but suppressed by institutional power before reaching an audience. Unlike all prior acoustic elements, which describe sounds that are produced and heard, the shelved frequency describes a sound that exists but cannot be heard -- the engine's first negative acoustic element. Alice Smith recorded an entire album for Epic Records between 2007 and 2012; that music exists in full fidelity but was never released. The shelved frequency is the resonance of those suppressed recordings. When Smith finally released She through Kickstarter in 2013, the shelved frequency was partially resolved -- the suppressed signal found an alternative transmission path.

ancestors: Silence (degree 0, Quincy Jones) -- the absence of sound by design. The shelved frequency inverts this: presence of sound, absence of transmission.
The Pacific crossing (degree 39, Ron Artis Jr) -- the most recent acoustic element. Geographic-timbral. The shelved frequency is institutional-timbral.
Dynamic grit (degree 37, Ayron Jones) -- technique-defined. The shelved frequency is suppression-defined.
genealogy.relationship: Forty-one acoustic elements across forty-one degrees. One per degree, no exceptions. The shelved frequency is the first negative element -- defined by what is not heard rather than what is heard. It marks the 41st element milestone. Track element 42 at degree 41.

theory_nodes.concept: The cultural satellite (forty-first artist-city pattern -- an artist who orbits a city's musical tradition without physically residing in it, connected through genre lineage, ideological alignment, and the gravitational pull of the city's musical identity; the engine's first non-geographic artist-city pattern; Alice Smith connects to Seattle through neo-soul genre lineage and Black Rock Coalition ideology, not residence or performance)
examples: Alice Smith (degree 40) -- D.C./NYC/LA-based. No documented Seattle residence or performance history. Connected to Seattle through: (1) neo-soul genre thread (degrees 18, 19, 25, 26, 40), (2) Black Rock Coalition ideology linking to Hendrix thread (degree 36), (3) Epic Records/Sony connection creating institutional bridge.
Contrast with The Returning Wave (degree 39, Ron Artis Jr) -- physical return visits creating deepening presence. The cultural satellite does not visit; it orbits.
Contrast with The Native (degree 4, Floyd Standifer) -- lifetime resident. The cultural satellite has no residential connection.

Contrast with all prior 40 patterns -- every previous pattern described a spatial relationship (residence, departure, return, migration, export, anchor). The cultural satellite describes a gravitational relationship -- connection through shared musical tradition rather than shared geography.
theory_nodes.description: The cultural satellite is the forty-first artist-city pattern in the Seattle 360 engine. It describes an artist who orbits a city's musical tradition without physically residing in it, connected through genre lineage, ideological alignment, and cultural gravity. Alice Smith is the first cultural satellite: her neo-soul practice connects to Seattle's neo-soul tradition (Maktub, Fly Moon Royale, Black Stax, Shaina Shepherd) through shared genre vocabulary; her Black Rock Coalition background connects to Seattle's Hendrix legacy through shared ideology; but she has no documented residential or performance connection to Seattle. This is the engine's first non-geographic artist-city pattern -- all forty prior patterns described spatial relationships.

ancestors: The Returning Wave (degree 39, Ron Artis Jr) -- physical returns creating presence. The cultural satellite orbits without visiting.
The Export (degree 0, Quincy Jones) -- departure from city. The cultural satellite never arrives.
The Touring Anchor (degree 38, Booker T. Jones) -- periodic visits from established base. The cultural satellite does not visit.
genealogy.relationship: Forty-one artist-city patterns across forty-one degrees. Eight PNW-to-global modes: Export (0), Incubator (30), Deep Root (35), Supernova (36), Inheritor (37), Touring Anchor (38), Returning Wave (39), Cultural Satellite (40). The cultural satellite is the first non-geographic mode.

theory_nodes.concept: Blue note -- the suppressed vibration (fortieth deployment; the blue note of an artist whose expression was institutionally blocked; present in the recording, absent from the public ear; defined by institutional constraint rather than by musical technique or geographic displacement; the Cabaret Prelude opening of She is the moment the suppressed vibration becomes audible)
examples: Alice Smith (degree 40) -- four-octave range containing blue notes at every register (gospel bends, soul vibratos, rock cries, falsetto precision). During Epic years, those blue notes were recorded but not released. The suppressed vibration is the blue note heard through institutional constraint.
The Cabaret Prelude (0:41 opening of She) -- the moment the suppressed vibration becomes audible after six years. A voice that has been waiting announces itself with theatrical deliberation.
Contrast with transoceanic bend (degree 39, Ron Artis Jr) -- geographic displacement. The suppressed vibration is institutional displacement.
First blue note in the engine defined by institutional constraint rather than by musical technique.
theory_nodes.description: Fortieth deployment of the blue note thread. The suppressed vibration is the blue note of an artist whose expression was institutionally blocked -- present in the recording, absent from the public ear. Alice Smith's four-octave range contains blue notes at every register, all of which were recorded for Epic Records and then shelved. The suppressed vibration is the tension between the note as it exists in the artist's body and the note as it fails to reach the audience. When She was finally released in 2013, the Cabaret Prelude (41 seconds of vocal introduction) became the moment of release -- controlled, theatrical, deliberate. The suppressed vibration is the first blue note defined by institutional constraint.

ancestors: Blue note -- transoceanic bend (degree 39, Ron Artis Jr): geographic displacement. The suppressed vibration is institutional displacement.
Blue note -- gospel swell (degree 38, Booker T. Jones): organ-based, sacred context. The suppressed vibration is voice-based, industry context.
All thirty-nine prior blue note deployments (degrees 0-39)
genealogy.relationship: Forty deployments across forty-one degrees. The suppressed vibration is the first blue note defined by institutional constraint. Track deployment 41 at degree 41.

theory_nodes.concept: Label-defiant tidal (ninth E=5 mode -- the energy of an artist who was suppressed by a major label, refused to conform, left the label system, rebuilt through independence, and emerged decades later performing at the highest level; adversarial tidal force: energy comes from pushing against the system that promised amplification; ninth consecutive E=5+ degree)
examples: Alice Smith (degree 40) -- E=5 label-defiant tidal. Grammy-nominated 2008. Signed to Epic Records. Shelved. Left label. Kickstarter-funded She (2013). Self-released Mystery (2019). Sinners soundtrack / Oscar performance (2025-2026). The arc from suppression to liberation to triumph generates E=5 energy.
Contrast with migratory tidal (degree 39, Ron Artis Jr, E=5) -- energy from geographic migration. Label-defiant energy comes from institutional resistance.
Contrast with institutional tidal (degree 38, Booker T. Jones, E=5) -- energy through institution. Label-defiant energy comes from pushing against institution.
Distribution after degree 40: 2:13:6:11:8:1. E=5 count rises to eight. Nine consecutive E=5+ (32-40).
theory_nodes.description: Label-defiant tidal is the ninth E=5 mode in the Seattle 360 engine. It describes the energy generated by an artist's resistance to institutional suppression -- the sustained effort of pushing against a major label that shelved completed work, refusing to conform to commercial formulas, leaving the label system, rebuilding through crowdfunding and independence, and ultimately returning to the industry on one's own terms. Alice Smith's E=5 at degree 40 reflects the accumulated momentum of a Grammy nomination, six years of shelved recordings, a Kickstarter-funded comeback, Prince covering her song, and an Oscar performance. The tidal force is adversarial: where migratory tidal (degree 39) draws energy from geographic movement, label-defiant tidal draws energy from institutional resistance.

ancestors: Migratory tidal (degree 39, Ron Artis Jr, E=5) -- energy from geographic migration. Label-defiant adds institutional resistance.
Institutional tidal (degree 38, Booker T. Jones, E=5) -- energy through institution. Label-defiant is energy against institution.
Breakthrough tidal (degree 35, Robert Cray, E=5) -- individual escape velocity. Label-defiant adds the specific mechanism of label resistance.
genealogy.relationship: Nine E=5 modes plus one E=6 mode across nine consecutive degrees (32-40). Progression: personal (32) -> scene (33) -> collective (34) -> breakthrough (35) -> transformative/E=6 (36) -> successor (37) -> institutional (38) -> migratory (39) -> label-defiant (40). Distribution: 2:13:6:11:8:1. Nine consecutive E=5+ degrees -- record extended.

theory_nodes.concept: Neo-Soul genre return (Genre Stage 21 -- neo-soul returns after 15-degree absence, the longest genre gap in the engine; degree 25 neo-soul was hip-hop-inflected and Central District-rooted; degree 40 neo-soul is rock-inflected and D.C.-rooted; same genre name, different sonic territory; genre evolution through name persistence and content transformation)
examples: Black Stax (degree 25) -- Neo-Soul (Hip-Hop, Soul, Blues, Spoken Word). Central District. Activist-driven community music. E=4.
Alice Smith (degree 40) -- Neo-Soul (Rock, Blues, Jazz, Gospel, Art-Pop). D.C./NYC/LA. Genre-fluid singer-songwriter music. E=5.
15-degree gap (26-39): genre progressed through Neo-Soul/R&B, Funk/Soul, Jazz/Funk, R&B/Soul, Soul/Gospel, Jazz/R&B, R&B, Blues, Blues Rock, Blues/Soul before returning to Neo-Soul.
Same genre name, different content. Genre evolution through the lens-refraction mechanism documented at degree 39.
theory_nodes.description: Neo-Soul returns at degree 40 after a 15-degree absence (last seen at degree 25, Black Stax), marking Genre Stage 21. This is the longest gap between appearances of the same genre in the engine. The return is structurally significant because the neo-soul at degree 40 is substantively different from the neo-soul at degree 25: Black Stax's neo-soul was hip-hop-inflected, Central District-rooted, and activist-driven; Alice Smith's neo-soul is rock-inflected, D.C.-rooted, and genre-fluid. The genre name persists while the content transforms -- the same lens-refraction mechanism documented at degree 39 (genre refraction), applied across fifteen degrees of genre evolution.

ancestors: Neo-Soul (degree 25, Black Stax) -- prior appearance. Hip-hop-inflected. 15-degree gap.
Blues/Soul (degree 39, Ron Artis Jr) -- Genre Stage 20. Blues/soul bridge.
Neo-Soul/R&B (degree 26, Shaina Shepherd) -- Genre Stage 9. Neo-soul compound.
genealogy.relationship: Twenty-one genre stages across forty-one degrees. Neo-Soul has appeared at degrees 25, 26 (compound), and 40. The 15-degree gap (26-40) passed through eight different genre zones before returning. Track at degree 41.

seattle_connection: CULTURAL SATELLITE -- Alice Smith (degree 40) is the engine's first artist connected to Seattle through genre lineage and ideological alignment rather than geography. Neo-soul genre thread links degrees 18, 19, 25, 26, 40. Black Rock Coalition connection links to Hendrix (degree 36). No documented Seattle residence or performance. THE SHELVED FREQUENCY as 41st acoustic element -- the engine's first negative element, defined by institutional suppression (Epic Records shelved her album 2007-2012). THE CULTURAL SATELLITE as 41st artist-city pattern -- the first non-geographic pattern, connection through genre gravity. THE SUPPRESSED VIBRATION as 40th blue note deployment -- defined by institutional constraint. LABEL-DEFIANT TIDAL as 9th E=5 mode -- energy from resistance to label suppression. NEO-SOUL RETURNS at Genre Stage 21 after 15-degree absence (longest genre gap). EPIC RECORDS as first Sony-family major label. SEVENTH WOMAN (7:34, ~17.1%). NINE CONSECUTIVE E=5+ (32-40). Distribution 2:13:6:11:8:1. Grammy-nominated (2008). Prince covered 'Another Love' (2014). Oscar performer (2026). Four-octave vocal range. Black Rock Coalition member. Label arc: BBE -> Epic (shelved) -> Kickstarter -> self-released -> Sony Masterworks. GENDER: 7:34 ratio (~17.1%). Female. 40TH DEGREE MILESTONE: first 1/9 of the circle complete.

theory_genealogy: The shelved frequency (NEW at degree 40): 41st acoustic element. First negative element -- defined by institutional suppression. Track element 42 at degree 41.
The cultural satellite (NEW at degree 40): 41st artist-city pattern. First non-geographic pattern -- connection through genre gravity. Eighth PNW-to-global mode.
Blue note -- the suppressed vibration (NEW at degree 40): 40th deployment. First blue note defined by institutional constraint. Track deployment 41.
Label-defiant tidal (NEW at degree 40): 9th E=5 mode. Energy from resistance to label suppression. Track.
Neo-Soul genre return (NEW at degree 40): Genre Stage 21. 15-degree gap. Longest genre absence in the engine. Track.
engine_sequencing: E=5 CONTINUES at 40: Distribution 2:13:6:11:8:1. Label-defiant tidal as 9th E=5 mode. NINE consecutive E=5+ degrees (32-40). Track at degree 41.
GENRE Stage 21 at degree 40: Neo-Soul returns. 15-degree gap (25-40). Rock-inflected vs hip-hop-inflected. Track at degree 41.
EPIC RECORDS established at 40: First Sony-family major label. Documents label-suppression pattern. Track label thread.
SHELVED FREQUENCY as 41st acoustic element: First negative element. Track element 42 at degree 41.
CULTURAL SATELLITE as 41st artist-city pattern: First non-geographic pattern. Track at degree 41.
NINE CONSECUTIVE E=5+ degrees (32-40): Record extended. The entire blues/soul/R&B/neo-soul zone operates at E=5+. Track at degree 41.
All engine sequencing items from degrees 0-39
cultural_patterns: Artist-city taxonomy: FORTY-ONE named patterns (adding The Cultural Satellite at degree 40). Eight PNW-to-global modes. Classify every degree.
Gender ratio: 7:34 (women:men). Alice Smith = female. ~17.1%. Highest representation to date. Track.
EPIC RECORDS / SONY FAMILY: First major-label-subsidiary appearance. Label-suppression narrative documented. Track.
BLACK ROCK COALITION connection: Links degree 40 to degree 36 (Hendrix) through genre-freedom ideology for Black artists.
NEO-SOUL RETURNS: 15-degree gap. Longest genre absence. Rock-inflected variant vs hip-hop-inflected variant.
LABEL-FREEDOM DIALECTIC: Full arc documented (independent -> major -> shelved -> crowdfunded -> independent -> selective re-engagement).
E=5 AS SUSTAINED MAXIMUM: Nine consecutive E=5+ degrees (32-40). Nine E=5 modes plus one E=6 mode. Track.
All cultural pattern items from degrees 0-39
